researchInterests: Integrated nanophotonics, quantum photonic sources, nonlinear on-chip devices, physics-informed inverse design, and wave-equation-constrained neural networks for photonic device optimization.

education.degree: B.Sc. in Electronics and Communications Engineering
bullets: Core curriculum: integrated photonics, electromagnetic theory, optical fiber communications, and digital signal processing.
Awarded Dean's Honors for Academic Excellence every year from 2021 to 2025.
Conducting device-level FDTD simulations for spontaneous parametric down-conversion (SPDC) sources on emerging material platforms.
Contributing to co-authored work on terahertz bound-state-in-the-continuum (BIC) metasurfaces, published in the Journal of Optics.

researchExperience.org: Advanced Labs Program, Egypt Scholars Inc.
bullets: Developed physics-informed neural network (PINN) models for wave-equation-constrained inverse design of NbOCl\textsubscript{2}-based SPDC sources.
Trained and benchmarked a 12-million-parameter PINN model against full-wave electromagnetic solvers; work submitted as a preprint.

researchExperience.org: OPST Group, SmartCI Research Center, Alexandria University
bullets: Modeled Fiber Bragg Grating (FBG) mode coupling and validated transfer-matrix results against full-wave FDTD simulations.
Contributed to the design of an FBG-based vital-sign monitoring sensor using coupled-mode and circuit-level analysis.

technicalSkills.items: Full-wave FDTD/FEM solvers, semiconductor drift-diffusion transport, coupled-mode theory, transfer-matrix method, waveguide dispersion engineering.
Python (NumPy, SciPy, Matplotlib), PyTorch, TensorFlow, Git, Linux, LaTeX.

awards.award: Huawei ICT Skills Competition -- National Finalist (AI Track)
NASA Space Apps Challenge -- Global Nominee

leadership.org: IEEE Solid-State Circuits Society (SSCS), Alexandria University Student Branch Chapter
bullets: Led chapter activities, organized technical seminars, and coordinated with IEEE Region 8 for student events.

memberships.name: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE)

references.name: Dr. Omar Abdelraouf

tools.title: NanoPhotoNet-MPM: Physics-Informed Neural Surrogate for Inverse SPDC Design
tools.objective: A physics-informed neural surrogate framework combining an EigenmodeDeepONet transverse eigensolver, a deterministic physics conversion layer, and a CWE-PINN longitudinal propagator for inverse design of modal phase-matched biphoton quantum light sources in anisotropic monoclinic NbOCl2 waveguides.
tools.methods: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs), DeepOperator Networks (DeepONet), coupled-wave equations, finite-difference eigenmode (FDE) solvers, genetic algorithm optimization, and joint spectral amplitude (JSA) analysis.

tools.title: Ge-on-Si PIN Photodetector Design Kit
tools.objective: A cascaded multiphysics simulation workflow for a waveguide-integrated Ge-on-Si vertical n-i-p photodiode targeting O-band intra-data-center optical links.
tools.methods: Finite-Difference Time-Domain (FDTD) optical absorption modeling, drift-diffusion electrical transport simulation, transit-time and RC bandwidth analysis, compact-model parameter extraction, system-level link simulation, and layout validation.
